Off Calendar May-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-25-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-07-2020 Continued To Jan-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: William Taylor appeared in court for Chao Tan.

Mr. Taylor requested a 30-day continuance stating that there is a related civil law suit, that there was a mediation which resulted in the dismissal of the civil case, that no assets went to the estate and that he understands that he needs to file a petition to terminate the estate for lack of assets. Status on close of estate is continued to January 25, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition is to be filed by January 4, 2021.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-26-2020 Continued To Dec-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-20-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: William Taylor for and with Chao Tan (Petitioner).
Ryan Meckfessel for Justin Cheung, Vilma Cheung and Celvin Cheung (Interested Persons).

Mr. Meckfessel stated that his Client is not objecting and that their only concern is the languishing civil action that is going to be triggered by the resolution of this petition. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required at this point. Status on close of estate is set for September 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Sep-17-2018 Continued To Nov-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jay Greene for and with Chao Hong Tan (Petitioner).
Ryan Meckfessel for Justin Cheung, Kelvin Cheung and Vilma Cheung (Interested Parties).

Mr. Greene stated that the civil action was filed first, that the motion to consolidate needs to be re-filed and that he will most likely get a hearing date in late October. The Court informed Counsel that he needs to file the motion to consolidate in the civil action in the Law & Motion Department, that the rule says the first case should be the lead case and that he needs to ask for the probate matter to be consolidated into the civil case. The Petition for Spousal Property is continued to November 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
